log_entries.thought_preview: The sample is a PE32 executable, likely a spreader with runtime modules, and has been analyzed in three different sandboxes. I should investigate the network activity to see if any suspicious communic

The network activity observation shows no DNS lookups, HTTP conversations, or IP traffic, which suggests that the malware may not be communicating with external servers. I should investigate the regis

The malware has used the AppInit_DLLs registry key for persistence, which is a common technique used by malware to load a DLL at system startup. I should investigate the dropped files to see if any ot
The dropped file is an executable, likely the initial payload dropped by the malware. I should investigate the process activity to see if any suspicious processes were spawned.

The process activity observation shows that no suspicious processes were spawned, but several notable modules were loaded. I should investigate the CAPA behaviors to see if any suspicious capabilities
The CAPA behaviors observation shows that the sample uses several suspicious capabilities, including linking functions at runtime and containing a resource section. I should investigate the IOC types

The IOC types observation shows that the sample uses registry persistence keys and drops a file, but no network activity or process creation was observed. I should now form a hypothesis about the TTPs
